page: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 virtual CPUs and 48 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz, supported by a 35 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 81317.48 and a PassMark CPU score of 55855.44, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 4502.42 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 97.63 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a PassMark database score of 21639.75 and Redis SET operations processing 74.14 million operations per second.

Despite strong multi-core capabilities, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1255.0 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression speed of 23.57 GB/sec. Large language model inference is also a bottleneck; text generation speeds for a medium 7B model drop to a poor 5.69 tokens per second due to the lack of GPU acceleration. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong database benchmark results, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, and caching layers rather than single-threaded processing or complex AI text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 48 physical cores. It lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying on a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core and memory performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 81317.48 and Redis SET operations reaching 74.14 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation is poor due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and high-throughput caching systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized x86_64 server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and strong database benchmark performance.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized configuration featuring ninety-six virtual CPUs and high-bandwidth memory architecture for intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory delivering high read and write bandwidth
Strong database performance with 74.14 million Redis SET operations per second
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and multi-core workloads
